title: Global Grain Trade Forecast Lowered Again
section_path: Global Grain Trade Forecast Lowered Again
section_path_text: Global Grain Trade Forecast Lowered Again
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) recently released their latest global grain export forecast for the 2022/23 season and has again reduced its expectations. The trade forecast is still very preliminary, but of note is that 486.6 million tons of exports are now expected. This is 800,000 tons less than was forecast a month ago and would mark a year-on-year decline of 22 million tons (-4%).
